channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US President Announces Temporary Ceasefire Between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, set to run from Saturday, May 9 to Monday, May 11, coinciding with Russia's Victory Day celebrations. The ceas...

item.title: Malema calls for immediate impeachment process following Constitutional Court ruling
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema celebrated outside the Constitutional Court in Braamfontein after the court ruled in favour of referring the Phala Phala matter back to Parliament. He announced that he would ...

item.title: Chris Mickelson set to make ACT Brumbies Super Rugby debut
item.description: Chris Mickelson, a Canberra rugby talent who has been with the Uni-Norths Owls since he was a child, is set to make his Super Rugby debut for the ACT Brumbies against the Western Force this Saturday n...

item.title: Ian Watkins faced threats and bullying in prison prior to stabbing incident, court told
item.description: Court proceedings have revealed that Ian Watkins, the former lead singer of the band Lostprophets and convicted paedophile, experienced bullying and threats from other inmates while serving his prison...

item.title: Notable Nigerian Music Releases from February to April 2026
item.description: For the first quarter of 2026, Afrobeats no too blow like previous years, but some solid music projects still drop wey deserve your ear. Shoday released 'Hybrid', him debut album wey get early singles...

item.title: Labour MPs discuss leadership ahead of May elections amid calls for Starmer to set departure timeline
item.description: In the lead-up to the forthcoming May elections, prominent Members of Parliament from the mainstream Labour Party convened to address growing internal pressure regarding the party's leadership. Allies...

item.title: Highway 1 in California fully reopens after three-year closure
item.description: California’s iconic Highway 1, a road famed for its dramatic cliffs and Pacific views, has reopened after being closed for three years due to landslides. Stretching along the Big Sur coast, the route ...

item.title: Katsina Governor raises alarm over security leaks and insider informants
item.description: Katsina State Governor, Dikko Umar Radda, don raise serious wahala over how bandits dey get government security info quick-quick after meetings. According to am, some people inside communities and eve...

item.title: Federal Government Proposes Reversing Pipeline Approval Process to Prioritise Cabinet Decision
item.description: The federal government of Canada is considering a significant change to the pipeline approval process. Under the proposed plan, cabinet would have the authority to approve new pipeline projects before...

item.title: Five-year-old boy found dead in Nyamira tea plantation after going missing from school
item.description: Police in Borabu Sub-county, Nyamira County, are investigating the tragic death of a five-year-old boy, Ezra Momanyi, whose body was discovered in a tea plantation a day after he failed to return home...

item.title: Kebbi Residents Alerted on Flood and Dry Spell Risks for 2026 Rainy Season
item.description: The National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) for Sokoto zone don alert people of Kebbi State say dem fit face wahala of flooding and dry spell ahead of the 2026 rainy season. According to NEMA, bas...

item.title: Alberta Whisky Act Supports Bow Valley Distillery and Local Tourism
item.description: The recent passage of the Alberta Whisky Act is being welcomed by the Bow Valley Distillery as a meaningful step for the local spirits industry. The legislation is designed to support small distilleri...

item.title: Record-breaking home sales highlight San Francisco's luxury market boom
item.description: San Francisco's housing market is experiencing unprecedented price surges, particularly in the high-end segment. Luxury homes are selling for nearly double their listing prices in just days. For insta...

item.title: Jay Sonza and Erik Celiz Post Bail in Cyberlibel Case over Alleged Fake Medical Records
item.description: Former broadcaster Jay Sonza and social media personality Erik Celiz have posted bail in the cyberlibel case filed by the National Bureau of Investigation. The charges relate to their alleged posting ...

item.title: Diageo launches World Class Nigeria 2026 to find country’s top bartenders
item.description: Diageo don officially open World Class Nigeria 2026, wey na di local part of di world’s most respected bartending competition. Dem dey waka go Lagos, Abuja, and Port Harcourt to find di most fearless ...

item.title: Real Madrid faces internal turmoil ahead of crucial clash with Barcelona
item.description: Real Madrid is enduring an unprecedented period of internal discord as they approach their upcoming clash with Barcelona at the Camp Nou. Head coach Álvaro Arbeloa faces a dressing room divided by fig...

item.title: Former Mpumalanga teacher sentenced for fraud after using fake matric certificate
item.description: A 43-year-old former teacher from Amersfoort in Mpumalanga, Nomalanga Labane, has been sentenced after admitting to fraud linked to a fake matric certificate she obtained in 2003. According to the Haw...

item.title: Philippines to Host ASEAN Maritime Center to Promote Peaceful Navigation
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. clarified that the proposed ASEAN Maritime Center in the Philippines is not meant to challenge or confront any particular country. Speaking at a press confere...

item.title: Nvidia, Apple and Alphabet Reach Record Market Values Amid Rising US Stocks
item.description: In early trading on Wall Street, the world's three most valuable publicly traded companies—Nvidia, Alphabet, and Apple—each reached new record highs. By midday, their combined market value had surpass...

item.title: Johannesburg families urged to inspect graves after heavy rainfall causes soil sinking
item.description: Recent persistent rainfall in Johannesburg has caused some graves in city cemeteries to sink, the Johannesburg City Parks and Zoo (JCPZ) has confirmed. Thanduxolo Mendrew, the MD of JCPZ, explained th...
